title: "I Want To Be Found Faithful"  II Timothy 4:8

notes.introduction: A sermon on 2 Timothy 4:8 examining Paul's declaration that a crown of righteousness is laid up for him, preached against the backdrop of Paul's imprisonment in the Mamertine Prison under Nero and his imminent martyrdom.
outline: I. Reaching precedes grasping: Paul's lifelong pursuit of faithfulness as the necessary foundation for receiving the crown of righteousness, illustrated through his three statements in vv. 6-7 (fighting the good fight, finishing the course, keeping the faith)
II. Obeying precedes rewarding: the crown as the result of obedience to each believer's individual God-given calling, not of superior human effort, with God as the righteous judge watching each life
III. Occupying precedes appearing: the command to use God-given gifts in service until Christ's return, contrasted with the example of Demas who forsook Paul having loved the present world (v. 10)
